key_theorem.statement: A real α is computable iff its digit function n↦d_n(α) is EML-finite. α is Martin-Löf random iff its digit function is EML-∞.
key_theorem.proof_sketch: Forward: if α computable, digit algorithm is a finite program → can be expressed as EML tree of bounded depth (each arithmetic op is EML-1 or EML-2). Backward: if digit function is EML-k for some k, then each digit d_n = round(EML_tree(n) mod base) is computable from n → α is computable. For Martin-Löf random α: any EML-finite f would give a computable approximation, but ML-random sequences are not computable → contradiction → must be EML-∞.

examples.why: arctan via BBP → EML-3 oscillatory series

computable_reals.name: π
computable_reals.description: Circle constant; computable via BBP formula

computable_reals.name: √2
computable_reals.description: Square root of 2; algebraic, computable via Newton's method

computable_reals.name: ln 2
computable_reals.description: Natural log of 2; EML-2 (output of ln gate)

chaitin_omega.reason: No EML-finite formula for Omega's digits; left-computable but not right-computable

normal_numbers.name: Champernowne C₁₀
normal_numbers.construction: Concatenate naturals: 0.123456789101112... — provably normal in base 10

normal_numbers.name: Copeland-Erdős
normal_numbers.construction: Concatenate primes: 0.2357111317... — provably normal in base 10

normal_numbers.name: π (conjectured)
normal_numbers.construction: π is conjectured normal but not proved; digits computed via EML-3 arctan/BBP

normal_numbers.name: Typical real (Borel)
normal_numbers.construction: Almost every real is normal; a generic normal real has no finite description → EML-∞

normal_numbers.name: Liouville constant
normal_numbers.construction: L = Σ 10^{-n!} is computable but NOT normal (digits 0 almost always)

champernowne_analysis.zero_crossings_comment: Many crossings → frequency oscillates around 1/10 → EML-∞ behavior in limit; but Champernowne itself is EML-2 because digit function is computable

martin_lof_random_sequence.reason: No finite EML tree generates a truly random sequence

chaitin_omega.reason: ML-random by definition; left-computable but not right-computable

normal_number_generic.description: Typical Borel-normal real — no explicit construction
normal_number_generic.reason: No EML-finite description; generic element of full-measure set

computable_real_eml1.reason: e = exp(1), exp(r) for rational r — single EML-1 atom

computable_real_eml3.reason: π via arctan = EML-3; erf^{-1} etc.

pseudorandom_sequence.description: Deterministic sequence that passes statistical tests
pseudorandom_sequence.reason: Generated by EML-2 recurrence (LCG, Mersenne); statistically random but EML-finite

normal_champernowne.reason: Digit function n↦d_n is rational arithmetic → EML-2, but distribution is uniform

kolmogorov_complexity_k.description: Kolmogorov complexity K(x) as a function of x
kolmogorov_complexity_k.reason: K is non-computable (halting problem); its values jump infinitely → EML-∞

eml_depth_as_complexity.description: EML depth as a computable proxy for Kolmogorov complexity
eml_depth_as_complexity.reason: EML-k depth is computable (bounded search); K(x) is not. EML depth ≈ log(K(x))

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: Periodic sequences (010101...), ultimately periodic binary strings
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Algebraic reals (√2, ∛3), ln(rational), Champernowne, Copeland-Erdős, PRNGs
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Martin-Löf random reals, Chaitin Ω, Kolmogorov complexity K(x), generic Borel normals

connections.to_session_40: EML Weierstrass → EML-finite functions are dense; random functions are EML-∞
connections.to_session_57: Boltzmann factor = EML-1 deterministic; thermal noise = EML-∞
connections.to_session_62: NS blowup = EML-∞ at T*; same barrier as algorithmic randomness
connections.to_turing: Halting problem undecidability ↔ EML-∞ of Chaitin Ω
